verbGlossary.BUFFERS AGAINST: {source} serves as a physical buffer that reduces exposure to {target} category (e.g., laydown area buffers against supply chain risk by staging materials on-site ahead of need)
verbGlossary.CONCENTRATES: {source} type creates a physical density of {target} due to work congestion, trade overlap, or hazard proximity (e.g., core shaft concentrates coordination risk where mechanical, electrical, and plumbing runs converge)
verbGlossary.CONSTRAINS: {source} imposes physical or sequencing limits that intensify {target} (e.g., work zone phase area constrains schedule risk by limiting concurrent trade access)
verbGlossary.EXPOSES TO: {source} characteristics create vulnerability to {target} category due to site conditions or physical properties (e.g., below-grade level exposes project to geotechnical risk from soil and water table conditions)
verbGlossary.FEEDS: {source}-based metric provides measured data that directly inputs into {target} assessment calculation (e.g., defect density by location feeds risk score by quantifying quality failure patterns)
verbGlossary.HOSTS: {source} is the physical container where risk items are tracked and managed (e.g., project site hosts {target} register as the geographic scope of all identified risks)
verbGlossary.MAPPED TO: {source} overlay corresponds to {target} classification for cross-domain compliance tracking (e.g., fire compartment mapped to risk category for code compliance and life safety)
verbGlossary.QUANTIFIES: {source}-based measurement assigns a numeric value to the magnitude of {target} (e.g., safety incident density by zone quantifies safety risk by measuring event frequency per area)
verbGlossary.SIGNALS: {source}-based metric serves as an early warning indicator that {target} is emerging or worsening in a specific area (e.g., punch items per room signals construction execution risk when counts exceed thresholds)
verbGlossary.SUBJECT TO: {source} falls under regulatory, permitting, or jurisdictional requirements that create compliance {target} (e.g., right-of-way is subject to regulatory and permit risk from municipal encroachment rules)
verbGlossary.SURFACES: {source}-based commissioning or inspection activity reveals previously hidden residual {target} (e.g., commissioning zone surfaces residual risk when functional testing uncovers latent defects)
